title: symphyotrichum pilosum var. pringlei
intro: Symphyotrichum pilosum var. pringlei, also known as Pringle's New England aster, is a perennial plant species in the daisy family that is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its essential oils and terpenoids. The health benefits of Symphyotrichum pilosum var. pringlei include relief from respiratory issues, such as congestion and coughs, as well as wound healing and skin protection. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
